name: Lemon Butter Layers
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------LEMON CREAM FILLING----------------------------
4 ounces Lemon pudding and pie mix
2 cups Water
2/3 cup Sugar
3/4 cup Butter
---------------------------------NUT CRUNCH---------------------------------
1/2 cup Sugar
3/4 cup Walnuts or almonds
1. Sift together flour, baking powder, and salt.
2. Cream butter with lemon extract until well combined.
3. Gradually add sugar, creaming for 5 to 8 minutes (the more you beat, the better the cake).
4. Blend in eggs one at a time; beat for 1 minute after each addition.
5. Add the dry ingredients and blend thoroughly.
6. Turn batter into a greased 9 or 10-inch tube pan.
7. Bake at 325°F for 55 to 60 minutes until the cake springs back when touched lightly in the center (do not invert).
8. Cool completely.
1. Cut the cooled cake horizontally to make 4 layers.
2. Stack the layers, spreading lemon cream filling between each layer and on top.
3. Prepare lemon cream filling by mixing the pudding mix with water according to package instructions, then adding sugar and butter. Beat until well combined and cool thoroughly.
4. Make nut crunch topping by heating sugar in a small skillet until it melts and becomes golden brown. Stir in walnuts or almonds. Spread on greased cookie sheet, let cool, and crush finely using a hammer or rolling pin.
DO NOT INVERT the cake while baking; avoid crushing too much during preparation to prevent crumbs from mixing into the layers.
